old_claim.note: Copper Wiring Production provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Insulated conductor windings around the soft-iron field structure and rotating armature enabled the self-exciting current-feedback circuit.

source_shape.source_locator: Pages 368-369 specify insulated wire around the soft-iron field and rotating armature; Wheatstone's parallel machine gives 640 feet of insulated copper field wire and 80 feet on the armature.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The self-excited machines used insulated conductor windings on both their field structures and rotating armatures.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The winding circuits carry the generated current back through the electromagnets, making electrical conductor fabrication a direct enabling input rather than generic industrial context.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved and strengthened: copper_wiring_production remains enabling with primary-source component evidence.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to a dynamo design that does not use wound field or armature conductors.
The cited apparatus is shown to use no insulated conductor windings.
The copper-wiring node is narrowed exclusively to later building wiring and excludes machine coils.
short_reason: Insulated windings are documented machine components.
